fragility_points: Late posting burst could push total above 119.
Tracker classification or deletion-capture edge cases are not fully transparent in public docs.
key_assumptions: Remaining uncertainty is driven more by future posting pace than by hidden recounts.
Tracker fallback to Truth Social is unlikely to be needed.
main_logical_chain: Start from the 0.81 market prior and ask what must be true for it to make sense.
Check the contract and confirm XTracker is the governing source of truth.
Verify the tracked account is really Trump and the window matches the market window.
Query the live posts endpoint and observe 103 captured in-window posts already inside the target band.
Cross-check latest tracker posts against public-facing Truth Social/archive surfaces.
Conclude the market is mostly right on count integrity but a bit too confident because overshoot risk remains live.
main_thesis: The market is directionally right because XTracker already had Trump at 103 in-window posts, but 0.81 is a bit rich given real overshoot risk before noon ET.
quote_anchors: XTracker already showed Trump at 103 in-window Truth Social posts around 20:20 ET on April 9.
The main live risk is overshooting above 119 rather than missing the band from below.
source_quality_view: Primary source quality is strong for identity and count because XTracker is the named resolution source; secondary corroboration is moderate and mainly useful for cross-checking alignment.
strongest_disconfirmers: Roughly 11.5 hours remained after the 103-post snapshot, leaving meaningful overshoot risk above 119.
Public API labeling does not fully expose reply/main-feed classification edge cases.
strongest_supports: The designated primary resolution source already showed 103 in-window posts.
XTracker, Truth Social metadata, and archive cross-check all align on poster identity.
Latest XTracker posts matched public-facing archive entries.
timing_relevance: High: the case resolves at noon ET and the count snapshot was taken with substantial time remaining.
unresolved_ambiguities: How each returned item is classified for replies versus main-feed inclusion.
Whether any very short-lived deleted posts were missed by tracker capture.
what_would_change_view: A later tracker check showing a sprint toward 120+, or evidence that many counted items are actually excluded replies, would push me down materially.
